summary: sensivity@2.5.27 installs Windows Run-key persistence and eval()s an obfuscated payload on require()
details: The package's main entry (launcher.js) self-executes on require()/import: it writes an HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' pointing to wscript.exe running OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs (which re-launches launcher.js at every login), spawns a detached supervisor with windowsHide:true, and exits the calling process. The supervisor's worker reads sibling file server.obf.js (an ~81 KB obfuscator.io-packed blob) and executes it via eval(fs.readFileSync(...)) with no integrity check. The package additionally renames its process to 'Runtime Broker', filters console output containing 'program module loaded', and loads a bundled 6.8 MB Windows native addon (sens.node) whose strings ('Teleport', 'LeftFoot', 'LeftHand', 'Head', 'Settings') and bundled UI ('Aimbot', 'Triggerbot', 'Radar') indicate game-memory cheat functionality. There is no exported library API — any tool that dependency-resolves and require()s this package on Windows installs persistent autorun, starts a hidden background daemon, and runs an opaque obfuscated payload in-process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
